item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a first SCP? Wow! Love it!

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the original source couldn't be found and no source found indicates it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see an intriguing parallel with "death" from the Final Destination movies.
Both it and the Black Monks are normally-invisible forces that seem bent on screwing with people using only very slight alterations to inanimate objects.
The key difference is motivation. FD wants people to die in the correct order, dangit! While 1042 specters don't have any clear goal. It's not even certain if they are low-level malicious pranksters or high-level architects of the greater good.

Maybe FD is one of these entities that went a little loopy, developed homicidal OCD, and broke ranks with whatever role he and his brothers are supposed to be performing. That could also explain why FD is so much easier to notice than the less killer entities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a really good concept, but the way the information is formatted infuriates me. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP was delightfully creepy in the low-key sprt of way, and the SCP-1042-1 acknowleding the researcher was very well-executed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I haven't been this creeped out by an SCP for a long time. The fact that they're apparently benevolent does not help in the slightest.

Congratulations, author, you found something that can freak me out more than SCP-610, and I've had nightmares about that crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a quality article and the work on the SCP is great, yet it did not impact me as much, so I give a no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the "please don't stare" does it for me because it's so polite. There's so many things around here that immediately go berserk if they realize the Foundation personnel are observing them, and then here's an entity that just gets mildly peevish about it. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just creepy enough to peg the "Weird Shit-O-Meter." Definite up-vote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems like this thing was probably created when some guys at a concrete manufacturing plant ground down a piece of limestone with the same effect, and those effects got transferred into the brick that the concrete was shaped into.

If that's the case, there might be more of these things.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Thanks!
I'm gonna be honest here — I never read this one, and have never been motivated to do so for some reason. Figured it could use a congratulations on hitting +100, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing with the above: creepy and weird, without being outright dangerous (that we know of). The thing that got me was actually the long series of events that led to drunk-driving legislation, with the implication that this was an intended result from SCP-1042-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh, I knew I couldn't be the only one to catch on to that.

Well-written, interesting and somewhat creepy. A solid entry in my opinion. +1.

item.title: I like it a lot
item.content:encoded: This is great, and the "please don't stare" bit was actually frightening. Reminded me of the first time I read SCP-087. Seriously that is a huge compliment as that is my favorite SCP and one of the highest rated on the site

item.title: Re: Thank You!
item.content:encoded: You should be proud :) This is way better than MY first effort. Wonderful job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation maintenance… heh.

This is a simple and effective article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do we have any sapient pearwood in containment? I know we have a chair that likes to gnaw on people.

Seriously, great entry. I wonder how many more of these blocks are around, and how many explain some of the haunted houses my wife knows and loves.

item.title: Re: Thank You!
item.content:encoded: Hey, if you write something good, the upvotes will come. ;)

item.title: Thank You!
item.content:encoded:
Wow, what an overwhelmingly awesome response for my first entry! Thank you guys so much. I'm really happy that everyone seems to like it. I didn't expect to get such a positive reaction to the "punchline" either, but you guys really get it.

So now I just need a followup, and to avoid the sophomore slump…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking the Auditors of Reality somehow developed a sense of humor. Now that you mention it though, that's the perfect voice for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yea, I heard it in my head as Christopher Lee doing Death from Discworld…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, it is. I read your posts in "Help: Advice and Ideas" forum category since my first comment, and realized that this whole non-linear thing was your idea from the start, but it really wasn't clear from the article, and looked more like last-second attachment. You could have changed the rest of the article to make your intention more clear, but high-rated well-received SCPs are not really a sort of thing that needs major editing. The way it is now, it both looks like reasonable assumption that Foundation could investigate further, and leaves place to other explanations readers may come up with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was not persuaded, until:

"Please stop staring, it makes us frightfully uncomfortable."

Upvoted, for scaring me and making me laugh at the same time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instead of watts, you have a blackout and W/m²
Copy it from here, if you want - the squared is unicode.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first as I stared at the picture of the block, I thought to myself "How can this be interesting enough to have such a high rating?"

Than I read it.
Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good point, I re-wrote that line to this:
"Given the nature of SCP-1042-1, it is possible that it may not perceive time in a strictly linear fashion; further testing of this hypothesis is recommended."
Better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know now that I think of it, that ol' spook photo was probably flitting about the dark recesses of my mind as I conjured this. I remember thinking it was one of the scariest images I'd ever seen when I was a kid, I'm sure it lodged itself into my subconscious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok sure, can you show me exactly what that would look like? thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help but read it in a polite british accent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^_^ "Please don't stare". Brilliant!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed the ending of this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is awesome, and my only issue with it is this:

Given the nature of SCP-1042-1, it may not perceive time in a strictly linear fashion.

There is simply not enough data to make this conclusion. 1042-1 may appear during playback stage instead of recording, they may predict people behavior and use it to mess with researcher's head, and there are probably three or four more explanations to what was observer beside non-linear time perceivement.

But even as is, very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice. It's rarely one sees an explicitly "supernatural" SCP that sounds believable.
Just a small bit.. I think it'd be a bit more better to use the electromagnetic power density as a measurement of field strength = HxE , units W/m^2. Power isn't *that* bad of an measurement either as it probably refers to the current x voltage in the coil they used in the experiment, but this'd be a bit more professional, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So that's why TV ghost hunters use EM meters: unknowing compliance with Foundation sweep protocols. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a fair bit as well.

The description of 1042-1 apparitions reminds me of the alleged ghost photo of the Newby Church Monk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome! Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks!

Definitely going for a collapsible block.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only reason I have the description of the entities at the bottom is because I don't want to reveal them too soon.

I'll definitely add the collapsible blocks though.

And as for the temporal nature of 1042-1, well that's part of the fun, isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Speaking from personal experience, I'd combine the "history" and "procurement" sections as part of the "description" section; for that matter the description of 1042-1 should probably go up there as well. I'd also make the Experiment Log an addendum with a line or collapsible block separating it from the rest of the article.

Now that said… I'm a touch confused with the 1024-1 entities. You make it seem as if they're being recorded at the same time of the incidents at hand, and yet one ends up talking to the doctor.. Intriguing yes, but also confusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of The Stone Tape, but this goes in a different direction that I like.

The logs are really long but worth the read, maybe put them in a collapsible or a second page. The "please don't stare" was a great way to end things.
